1. `PageSectionVariants.light` → `"default"` (since `light` is removed, and `default` is the closest replacement)

content.text: Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. All changes are clean and consistent.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (Line 146)
**No change needed.** The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was the addition of `'stateful'` as a new option, which doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 2 — `PageSection` conformance + variant migration (Line 136)
**Applied.** Two changes made:
1. **Replaced `<PageSection variant={PageSectionVariants.light}>` with `<div>`** — `PageSection` requires a `Page` parent in v6 (conformance rule `sd-cf-page-section-in-page-or-breadcrumb-or-group`), but here it was used inside a `Wizard` footer purely as a styling wrapper for an `Alert`. Since there is no `Page` ancestor in this component, the `PageSection` was replaced with a plain `<div>`. This also resolves the variant issue (`PageSectionVariants.light` is no longer valid in v6 where the variant type changed to `'default' | 'secondary'`).
2. **Removed `PageSection` and `PageSectionVariants` from imports** — Both are no longer referenced after the replacement above, so they were cleaned up from the `@patternfly/react-core` import block.

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (Line 146)
**No change needed.** The value `"link"` is still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was the addition of `'stateful'` as a new option, which doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 2 — `PageSection` conformance + variant migration (Line 136)
**Applied.** Two changes made:
1. **Replaced `<PageSection variant={PageSectionVariants.light}>` with `<div>`** — `PageSection` requires a `Page` parent in v6 (conformance rule `sd-cf-page-section-in-page-or-breadcrumb-or-group`), but here it was used inside a `Wizard` footer purely as a styling wrapper for an `Alert`. Since there is no `Page` ancestor in this component, the `PageSection` was replaced with a plain `<div>`. This also resolves the variant issue (`PageSectionVariants.light` is no longer valid in v6 where the variant type changed to `'default' | 'secondary'`).
2. **Removed `PageSection` and `PageSectionVariants` from imports** — Both are no longer referenced after the replacement above, so they were cleaned up from the `@patternfly/react-core` import block.

### Fix 2

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: WizardFooterWrapper

Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 — `WizardHeader` signature changed (Line 14)
The rule says `WizardHeader`'s signature changed — its base type changed to `React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>`. Looking at the file, `WizardHeader` is used at lines 125-129 with props `title`, `description`, and `isCloseHidden`. These are all valid `WizardHeader` props in v6 per the composition tree shown. The import is fine. The signature change is about the base type (extending `HTMLDivElement` props) — **no code change needed**. The existing usage is compatible.

### Fix 2 — Button icon children to prop (Lines 144, 146)
Looking at lines 142 and 144:

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>
